top_signals.title: Israel Assets Plunge on Peace Talks After Years-Long War Rally
top_signals.summary: Israel’s stocks and currency have turned to the world’s worst performers this month on concern that a peace agreement with Iran will leave Israel in a weakened position with respect to both its biggest enemies and allies.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China Stocks in Hong Kong Set for Bear Market on Spending Woes
top_signals.summary: Chinese equities in Hong Kong headed toward a bear market as sentiment soured on tepid consumer spending and fading confidence in e-commerce firms.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Senegal Trade Minister Says Government Open to Debt Revamp
top_signals.summary: Senegal is open to a debt restructuring as mounting payment pressures threaten to strain key sectors of the economy, according to a senior member of President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s cabinet.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
